title: A Poem For My Mother By Robin Ngangom | Line By Line Explanation in Hindi
description:
Few poems dare to hold a mother accountable — yet Robin Ngangom's "A Poem For My Mother" does exactly that, unraveling a son's complicated love through images of violence, silence, and survival rooted in Northeast India's fractured world. This poem is increasingly central to BA English Honours syllabi across DU, IGNOU, and SOL, where students grapple with its unflinching honesty about familial pain and postcolonial identity. Ngangom writes not with sentimentality but with the precision of someone who has had to choose between loyalty and truth — and that tension is what makes this poem impossible to forget.
